anchor.snippet: a torus ( pl. : tori or toruses ) is a surface of revolution generated by revolving a circle
the surface has a ring shape and is called a torus of revolution
If the axis of revolution is tangent to the circle, the surface is a horn torus
If the axis of revolution passes twice through the circle, the surface is a spindle torus
If the axis of revolution passes through the center of the circle, the surface is a degenerate torus
If the revolved curve is not a circle, the surface is called a toroid
A solid torus is a torus plus the volume inside the torus

annotations.label: Ring torus is homeomorphic to S¹ × S¹

anchor.snippet: a ring torus is homeomorphic to the Cartesian product of two circles
This produces a geometric object called the Clifford torus
An example of a torus can be constructed by taking a rectangular strip of flexible material

annotations.label: Topological torus as product of two circles

anchor.snippet: a torus is a closed surface defined as the product of two circles

annotations.label: S³ as nested family of tori (Hopf bundle)

anchor.snippet: is filled out by a family of nested tori in this manner

The fundamental group of the torus is just the direct product of the fundamental group of the circle with itself
Its higher homotopy groups are all trivial
The torus has homology groups

annotations.label: First homology isomorphic to fundamental group

anchor.snippet: the first homology group of the torus is isomorphic to its fundamental group

annotations.label: Cohomology groups isomorphic to homology groups

anchor.snippet: The cohomology groups with integer coefficients are isomorphic to the homology ones

annotations.label: Punctured torus turned inside out yields another torus

anchor.snippet: If a torus is punctured and turned inside out then another torus results

annotations.label: 2-torus is a twofold branched cover of the 2-sphere

anchor.snippet: The 2-torus is a twofold branched cover of the 2-sphere, with four ramification points

annotations.label: Every conformal structure arises as such a cover

anchor.snippet: Every conformal structure on the 2-torus can be represented as such a two-sheeted cover of the 2-sphere
The points on the torus corresponding to the ramification points are the Weierstrass points
the conformal type of the torus is determined by the cross-ratio of the four points
the n -dimensional torus is topologically equivalent to the product of n circles
the n -torus is R n modulo the action of the integer lattice Z n

annotations.label: n-torus is a compact manifold and a compact abelian Lie group

anchor.snippet: An n -torus in this sense is an example of an n- dimensional compact manifold
in any compact Lie group G one can always find a maximal torus
Toroidal groups are examples of protori

annotations.label: Automorphisms of the torus from lattice automorphisms

anchor.snippet: Automorphisms of T are easily constructed from automorphisms of the lattice
The fundamental group of an n -torus is a free abelian group of rank n
The k th homology group of an n -torus is a free abelian group of rank n choose k
the Euler characteristic of the n -torus is 0 for all n
can be identified with the exterior algebra over the Z - module

annotations.label: n-torus as configuration space of ordered points on a circle

anchor.snippet: the n -torus is the configuration space of n ordered, not necessarily distinct points on the circle

annotations.label: Unordered configuration space is the orbifold Tⁿ/Sₙ

anchor.snippet: The configuration space of unordered , not necessarily distinct points is accordingly the orbifold

annotations.label: n = 2: quotient is the Möbius strip

anchor.snippet: For n = 2 , the quotient is the Möbius strip

annotations.label: n = 3: quotient is a triangular-prism solid torus with a twist

anchor.snippet: For n = 3 this quotient may be described as a solid torus with cross-section an equilateral triangle
A flat torus is a torus with the metric inherited from its representation as the quotient
